page: OVHcloud c3-32 is a compute-optimized server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 AMD EPYC processor clocked at 2.3 GHz, with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside 400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is supported by a 2 Gbps baseline bandwidth, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core metrics, falls within the middle 50% average. Text processing and bzip3 decompression (both single- and multi-threaded) show strong performance, while software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing benchmarks register average results. Memory performance is characterized by a strong PassMark memory score, though raw memory bandwidth read and write tests remain in the average tier.

This hardware profile is designed for compute-bound workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading, such as text processing, data decompression, and light LLM text generation using small models. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it less suitable for memory-intensive databases, though the 400 GB NVMe SSD supports local storage needs. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation provides predictable compute performance for CPU-heavy tasks, avoiding the resource contention typical of shared-core or burstable instances.
description: The OVHcloud c3-32 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, running at 2.3 GHz with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, 400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance, bzip3 decompression, and text processing capabilities, placing in the top 25% of tested servers. Conversely, multi-core operations, software compilation, and database operations perform in the average tier. Lacking hyperthreading and featuring a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is tailored for dedicated compute tasks, text processing, and light model inference rather than memory-heavy database deployments.
og_description: OVHcloud c3-32 is a compute-optimized KVM instance with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 32 GB RAM, and 400 GB NVMe storage, delivering strong single-core and decompression performance.

tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC compute instance featuring sixteen physical cores, local NVMe storage, and strong single-core processing performance.

32.0 GB of system memory and 400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU, text processing, and decompression performance
